text:
An Australian gymnast fought back tears as she revealed the devastating news she had suffered a serious injury which rules her out of the Paris Olympics.

An Australian gymnast has revealed her devastation at not being able to compete at this year's Olympics after suffering a serious injury less than three months out from the Paris games.

Seven-time national All Around champion Georgia Godwin fought back tears as she informed her legion of Instagram followers on Thursday that she would not be heading to her second Olympics in July.

Godwin was preparing for this year's national championships on the Gold Coast when her Olympic dreams were crushed.

"This is definitely not the news I want to be sharing with you all right now, a couple of days out from nationals, less than 80 days from Paris," she said in a video message.

"I've sustained an Achilles injury. It's extremely heartbreaking, it's not ideal.

"I'll be heading back to Brisbane, having a chat with the surgeon, we'll just see where we go from there.

"I'm not entirely sure what the recovery process looks like moving forward but I've got an amazing team around me, and (I'm) trying to be as positive as possible."

The 26-year-old played a crucial role in helping the Australian Women's Artistic Gymnastics team earn a spot in the 2024 Olympics through last year's world championships.

Taking to Instagram after the team qualified in October, Godwin said: "The Aussie WAG team are going to the Paris Olympics. I'm struggling to comprehend what I'm feeling right now but one thing I am sure of... I'm so bloody proud of this team".

Godwin, who won two gold medals at the 2022 Commonwealth Games, was one of nine athletes chosen in April to showcase Australia's opening ceremony uniforms.

Her injury follows on from Australia's top-ranked men's trampoline gymnast Blake Rutherford also suffering a serious injury in training, dashing his Olympics hopes.

"Gymnastics Australia is devastated for Georgia and Blake, and our staff are providing both athletes with the organisation’s full support," a statement said.

"The entire gymnastics community in Australia wishes them both well in their recovery."
